findings.issue: T9 double-starts the sender 'a', whose relay reads 'a's inbox and is irrelevant to delivery into 'b'; a duplicate-relay-thread regression would be invisible. Even on the receiver, two relay threads competing on one async-channel consume each item once (no duplication), so the 'no duplication' claim is not actually proven. Impl is correctly idempotent, so the AC passes; this is a test-coverage gap only.
findings.fix: Double-start the receiver 'b' and assert exact order over 3+ messages (received == '(1 2 3)); a split stream from competing readers would scramble order. Optional, non-blocking.

findings.issue: transport-start sets started?=#t before draining the pre-start queue. A concurrent send that observes started?=#t mid-drain can async-channel-put directly ahead of still-draining buffered items (FIFO inversion); the mirror case can append to a queue that is never drained (lost message). Not exercised by the single-threaded suite.
findings.fix: Document in Decisions that pre-start FIFO holds only when sends and the peer's transport-start are not concurrent, or serialize the started?-flip + drain under a lock. No code change required for this item.

findings.issue: racket/contract is required but unused (no define/contract or contract-out in the module). Permitted by AC-11, so not a violation, just dead weight.
findings.fix: Drop the (require racket/contract) line, or add it back when a contract-out provide is introduced.
